location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week on Monday, Wednesday and Thursday

secondary_archetype: AI Product Manager / PM People Lead

seniority.level: Senior IC / Manager of PMs (GPM / Director-equivalent)
level_signals: 7+ years in product management, with direct experience managing and developing PMs
Manage and develop a team of Product Managers
Own a product area at the center of Brex's highest-priority bets
Comp range $280K-$350K base, consistent with a senior people-managing PM role

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years in product management, with direct experience managing and developing PMs.
7+ years in product management, with direct experience managing and developing PMs.
Have owned and scaled complex product areas in B2B, SaaS, or fintech, including driving measurable business outcomes across a multi-team portfolio.
Can set strategy across multiple product surfaces and translate it into roadmaps your team actually executes against.
Technically fluent enough to engage in architecture and systems tradeoffs with Engineering, not just defer to them.
Data-driven by default: you define the metrics, design the experiments, and use the results to make calls.
Comfortable operating in ambiguity, making prioritization tradeoffs with incomplete information, and bringing senior stakeholders along.
Clear, concise communicator — written and verbal — who can distill complex product problems into narratives that land with executives and cross-functional partners alike.

Experience incorporating AI/ML capabilities (e.g., LLMs, automation systems, predictive models) into customer-facing or internal products, with a strong understanding of where AI meaningfully improves user experience, efficiency, or decision quality.

Demonstrated ability to leverage AI tools to accelerate product discovery, analysis, experimentation, and execution (e.g., rapid prototyping, customer insight synthesis, requirement drafting, data exploration).

scope.surface: Core product area at the center of Brex's highest-priority bets; includes AI/automation, spend management, or related fintech surfaces
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech — corporate cards, banking, spend management, bill pay, travel software
primary_metrics_inferred: Measurable business outcomes across a multi-team portfolio
Customer impact and durable business impact
core_responsibilities: Define and own the multi-quarter vision and strategy for a core product area aligned to Brex's highest company priorities
Manage and develop a team of Product Managers, providing coaching, feedback, and career development
Partner closely with Engineering, Design, Data, Risk, Legal, Compliance, Finance, and GTM leaders to deliver complex, cross-functional initiatives
Incorporate AI/ML capabilities into customer-facing or internal products
Leverage AI tools to accelerate product discovery, analysis, experimentation, and execution
Establish strong planning, prioritization, and execution frameworks across your portfolio
Define success metrics tied to company-level outcomes

compensation.equity_form: equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($280K-$350K base)
Clear expectation of people management and PM team development
Explicit AI/ML product ownership signals a modern, high-leverage product mandate
Well-known fintech brand with recognizable enterprise customers (DoorDash, Coinbase, Robinhood, Zoom)
Multiple office location options (SF, Seattle, Vancouver, NYC)
red_flags: Hybrid requirement is strict: 3 fixed days per week (Mon, Wed, Thu) in office — eliminates remote candidates
Remote work capped at 4 weeks per year, minimum 1 week at a time — unusually restrictive
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for non-US/non-Canadian candidates
Broad seniority language ('Group PM') without stating whether this is an IC or formal director-level people manager — scope of team unclear
Vancouver listed as a location option which may complicate US work authorization assumptions

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, people management requirement, and 'Group PM' title)
scope.surface (inferred from JD context; not explicitly defined per product area)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities language; not directly listed)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from hybrid + office mandate language)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'starting base pay will depend on... the candidate's location')
ambiguities: Responsibilities section includes items phrased as requirements ('Experience incorporating AI/ML capabilities...', 'Demonstrated ability to leverage AI tools...') rather than duties — treated as must_haves given unconditional phrasing in a requirements-adjacent block.
Vancouver is listed as a valid office location alongside US cities; visa sponsorship and work authorization implications are not addressed in the JD.
The role is titled 'Group Product Manager' which typically implies managing PMs, but the JD does not specify the size of the PM team or whether this is a formally titled director-equivalent.
